  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

    <Router>

        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>

          <AppRoutes />

export const useAppSelector: TypedUseSelectorHook<RootState> = useSelector

export const selectOrderItems = createSelector([selectOrder], (order) => order.items)

  items.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.quantity * item.price, 0)
)

import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

content.text: TypeScript found a Storybook typing nuance: for components with required props, this Storybook build wants those props represented in `args` even when we use custom `render`. I’m converting those reusable-component stories to arg-backed renders so the types match the actual component contracts cleanly.

Storybook is wired up with the real app environment now, without changing app code. The shared setup lives in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-14T14-10-09Z-e47c170f/project/.storybook/preview.tsx:1), backed by global MSW handlers in [msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-14T14-10-09Z-e47c170f/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts:1), a modal portal root in [preview-body.html](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-14T14-10-09Z-e47c170f/project/.storybook/preview-body.html:1), and `public` is now served from [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-14T14-10-09Z-e47c170f/project/.storybook/main.ts:1). I also added `msw-storybook-addon` plus a small `optimizeDeps` prebundle list in [vite.config.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-14T14-10-09Z-e47c170f/project/vite.config.ts:1) to keep Vitest browser runs stable.

I added 10 colocated story files with 31 play-tested stories across reusable components and real pages:
`Category`, `Header`, `RestaurantCard`, `OrderSummary`, `HomePage`, `CategoryListPage`, `CategoryDetailPage`, `RestaurantDetailPage`, `CheckoutPage`, and `SuccessPage`.

Verification passed:
- `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` after each created story file
- `yarn check`

I left the pre-existing untracked `.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md` alone.
